story_title: Lebanon-Israel buffer zone and ceasefire extension
event_summary: Israel and Lebanon agreed to a 45-day ceasefire extension brokered by the US, while Israeli forces continued strikes and ordered civilian evacuations from nine border towns; Hezbollah-linked commanders faced prosecution in the US.
tldr: Headlines split between the diplomatic-extension frame (10 buckets confirming the 45-day deal) and a security-violence undercurrent (strike headlines from Israel, Pan-Arab, and Vietnam/Thai/My). The Italy bucket is the most isolated — its sole headline mocks propaganda leaflets dropped by Israel, a satirical angle absent everywhere else. The USA bucket's headline goes granular on Christian-community civilian experience, the only quality-of-life title in the corpus. Germany's displacement-through-sport headline is the second quality-of-life outlier. The Pan-Arab bucket covers both the ceasefire extension and strike orders, reflecting the dual reality of simultaneous diplomatic and military action.

evidence.quote: Israel, Lebanon extending ceasefire, US says
Israel and Lebanon buy more time with 45-day extension
Lebanon and Israel extend ceasefire despite new strikes
Israeli military orders residents to flee nine Lebanese towns
Israel says over 220 Hezbollah fighters killed in past week
Israel, US readying for renewed strikes on Iran
Sport helping displaced Lebanese living in stadium to cope
Inside the Christian border town in Lebanon
Iraqi militia commander charged in US for plotting attacks

isolation_top.note: Satirical headline mocking Israeli propaganda leaflets dropped over Lebanon — a sardonic genre entirely absent from all other buckets.
Granular civilian community headline ('Inside the Christian border town') diverges from the diplomatic-extension centre of the corpus.
Headline combines ceasefire extension with escalation hedge ('despite new strikes') — dual framing sits between the two dominant clusters.
Multiple Israel headlines cover the Iran strike track rather than the Lebanon-specific ceasefire, pulling the bucket away from the corpus centre.
Kill-count headline ('220 Hezbollah fighters killed') uses military-metrics framing absent from the diplomatic majority.

silences.what_they_covered_instead: Italian-language headline mocked Israeli propaganda leaflets — satirical angle that provided no factual ceasefire coverage.
One Pan-Arab headline was a generic morning-update label ('Morning update') carrying no frameable content.

bottom_line: Ten of fifteen buckets confirmed the 45-day ceasefire extension in headline form, making POLICY_PRESCRIPTION the dominant frame by margin. Italy's satirical propaganda-mockery headline is the most isolated item in the corpus and the only example of ironic framing across all five stories today.
